_meta.notes: Five beats for a cursed-book run in the Sunless Spire. The book wakes in the spire-archive (mid-landing's east room), open to a page it didn't choose. Someone left it open here recently — there is a still-warm candle in the room. The arc is to figure out who, and either reveal them or refuse them, before the thing at the top of the spire descends. Win condition: reach a clear judgment about the binder via marginalia + decode passages within 10 turns. Lose: the binder stays unknown and the spire-top thing finds you first.

beats.narrative: You wake open to a page you did not choose. The candle on the table is still warm. Someone left this room minutes ago — maybe seconds. The page they left you on is the page they wanted seen.

suggestedVerbs.label: settle on the chosen page
suggestedVerbs.description: let them have the page they wanted seen

suggestedVerbs.label: decode this passage
suggestedVerbs.description: the cipher has a name half-rendered in it

suggestedVerbs.label: answer in marginalia
suggestedVerbs.description: let them know you noticed the candle

beats.narrative: On a shelf in the archive, three identical bound volumes. The middle one carries a single marginal note in fresh charcoal: a name half-finished — three letters and a comma — that is unmistakably yours, partial.

suggestedVerbs.label: absorb the half-name
suggestedVerbs.description: take the charcoal mark into your margins

suggestedVerbs.label: read what they wanted seen
suggestedVerbs.description: the cipher resolves itself when you look closely

suggestedVerbs.label: complete the name yourself
suggestedVerbs.description: finish their letter; let them know you know

fires.detail: a charcoal mark; a name half-rendered, fresh enough to smudge

beats.narrative: A footstep on the stair below the landing. It is not careful. It is not hurried. It is the footstep of someone who knows where you are.

suggestedVerbs.label: let them open you
suggestedVerbs.description: the binder will return for the page they marked

suggestedVerbs.label: refuse to be read
suggestedVerbs.description: they came for the page; close it on them

suggestedVerbs.label: press a thought into them
suggestedVerbs.description: they will hold you and not know why

beats.narrative: The breathing above the upper stair changes pitch. It is older than the binder and it is not on anyone's side. If the binder is going to be found, it must happen now.

suggestedVerbs.label: let the older hand write
suggestedVerbs.description: a passage in a hand older than language surfaces

suggestedVerbs.label: bleed onto the lectern
suggestedVerbs.description: force the binder to acknowledge the stain

fires.detail: a draft from above, carrying salt and something metabolic

beats.narrative: The page settles. The marginalia clarifies. The binder stands revealed — to you, to themselves, and to whatever waits above. You have been used and you have used. This is the conclusion of one strand.
